prompt:
Your task is to identify likely causal genes within a locus for a given GWAS phenotype. From the list, provide only the likely causal gene (matching one of the given genes). 
Identify the causal gene.
GWAS phenotype: Type 2 diabetes (type II diabetes mellitus)
Genes in locus: {AARD},{EIF3H},{MED30},{RAD21},{SLC30A8},{UTP23}
raw_response:
Thinking and reasoning:
SLC30A8 (ZnT8) is a well-established causal gene for type 2 diabetes: common and rare variants at this locus associate with T2D risk, and loss-of-function variants in SLC30A8 have been shown to be protective and to affect beta-cell function and insulin secretion. The other genes listed (AARD, EIF3H, MED30, RAD21, UTP23) do not have comparable genetic or functional evidence linking them to T2D. Based on genetic association, human LoF studies, and pancreatic islet expression, SLC30A8 is the likely causal gene in this locus.

Plan:
1. [✓] Review genetic and biological evidence for genes in the locus, focusing on SLC30A8 (completed)
2. [✓] Select the single most likely causal gene from the provided list (completed)

Answer:
<solution>SLC30A8</solution>
trajectory:
================================ Human Message =================================

Your task is to identify likely causal genes within a locus for a given GWAS phenotype. From the list, provide only the likely causal gene (matching one of the given genes). 
Identify the causal gene.
GWAS phenotype: Type 2 diabetes (type II diabetes mellitus)
Genes in locus: {AARD},{EIF3H},{MED30},{RAD21},{SLC30A8},{UTP23}

================================== Ai Message ==================================

Thinking and reasoning:
- SLC30A8 (ZnT8) is a well-known gene with multiple studies linking it to type 2 diabetes (T2D); rare loss-of-function variants in SLC30A8 have been reported to be protective for T2D, which strongly supports causality at loci where SLC30A8 is located. I will confirm this with quick literature and database queries and check tissue expression to support the biological plausibility (pancreatic islet expression). I will follow a short plan and run queries to PubMed, the GWAS Catalog, and expression (archs4) for the genes in the locus, then provide the single likely causal gene from the provided list.

Plan:
1. [ ] Query PubMed for evidence linking each gene (especially SLC30A8) to type 2 diabetes.
2. [ ] Query the GWAS Catalog for T2D associations at the locus and implicated gene.
3. [ ] Retrieve top tissue expression for each gene (archs4) to check pancreatic/islet expression.
4. [ ] Summarize findings and return the single likely causal gene from the provided list.

I will now run the queries and update the plan as I complete each step.

<execute>
# Stepwise queries to support selecting the causal gene.
from biomni.tool.literature import query_pubmed
from biomni.tool.genomics import get_rna_seq_archs4
from biomni.tool.database import query_gwas_catalog

# Show start of research log
print("Research log start: Identify likely causal gene in locus for Type 2 diabetes")

# 1) PubMed queries for SLC30A8 and other locus genes vs Type 2 diabetes
queries = {
    "SLC30A8": "SLC30A8 type 2 diabetes ZnT8 protective loss-of-function",
    "AARD": "AARD type 2 diabetes",
    "EIF3H": "EIF3H type 2 diabetes",
    "MED30": "MED30 type 2 diabetes",
    "RAD21": "RAD21 type 2 diabetes",
    "UTP23": "UTP23 type 2 diabetes"
}

pubmed_results = {}
for gene, q in queries.items():
    result = query_pubmed(query=q, max_papers=3)
    pubmed_results[gene] = result
    print(f"\n--- PubMed result for {gene} ---")
    print(result)

# Update plan after step 1
print("\nStep 1 completed: PubMed queries executed.")

# 2) GWAS Catalog query for Type 2 diabetes trait (look for SLC30A8 locus associations)
gwas_prompt = "Type 2 diabetes SLC30A8 locus"
gwas_result = query_gwas_catalog(prompt=gwas_prompt, max_results=5, genes=["SLC30A8","AARD","EIF3H","MED30","RAD21","UTP23"])
print("\n--- GWAS Catalog query result ---")
print(gwas_result)

print("\nStep 2 completed: GWAS Catalog queried.")

# 3) Get top tissue expression from archs4 for each gene to evaluate pancreas/islet expression
expr_results = {}
for gene in ["SLC30A8","AARD","EIF3H","MED30","RAD21","UTP23"]:
    res = get_rna_seq_archs4(gene_name=gene, K=5)
    expr_results[gene] = res
    print(f"\n--- archs4 top tissues for {gene} ---")
    print(res)

print("\nStep 3 completed: Expression queries executed.")

# Summarize minimal findings for selection (will be used by assistant after observe)
summary = {
    "pubmed_snippets": {g: (pubmed_results[g][:500] if isinstance(pubmed_results[g], str) else str(pubmed_results[g])) for g in pubmed_results},
    "gwas": gwas_result,
    "expression": {g: (expr_results[g][:500] if isinstance(expr_results[g], str) else str(expr_results[g])) for g in expr_results}
}
print("\nResearch log end.")
# Save outputs to variables so system captures them
result_pubmed = pubmed_results
result_gwas = gwas_result
result_expr = expr_results

print("\nSaved variables: result_pubmed, result_gwas, result_expr")
</execute>

--- PubMed result for SLC30A8 ---
Title: Loss-of-function variant of SLC30A8 rs13266634 (C > T) protects against type 2 diabetes by stabilizing ZnT8: Insights from epidemiological and computational analyses.
Abstract: The loss-of-function variant rs13266634 (C > T) in SLC30A8 has been linked to type-2-diabetes (T2D) susceptibility with conflicting findings. We investigated its role among T2D patients of Bangladesh. The rs13266634 (C > T) variant results in an arginine-to-tryptophan (p.R325W) substitution of SLC30A8-encoded zinc transporter protein, ZnT8. As zinc is crucial for insulin-crystallization for storage, we analyzed its wild-type (WZnT8) and variant (MZnT8) protein conformations via molecular-dynamics (MD) simulations. For the epidemiological analysis, a case-control study was conducted with 90 T2D patients from a diabetic-patient-cohort and 90 gender-matched healthy-controls. Genomic DNA was isolated from blood samples and genotyped by Sanger sequencing. MD simulations, spanning over 100 ns, were executed using GROMACS v2024.4. The homozygous wild CC-genotype was significantly more frequent in T2D patients (66.7 %) than in control (50.0 %) and was increasing T2D risk, with adjusted odds-ratio (OR
Journal: Journal, genetic engineering & biotechnology

Title: Complete loss of SLC30A8 in humans improves glucose metabolism and beta cell function.
Abstract: Genetic association studies have demonstrated that partial loss of SLC30A8 Function protects against type 2 diabetes in humans. We investigated the impact of complete loss of SLC30A8 Function on type 2 diabetes risk and related phenotypes in humans.
The Pakistan Genome Resource (PGR), a biobank comprising whole-exome and whole-genome sequences of 145,037 participants, was analysed for phenotypic associations with SLC30A8 loss-of-function (LoF) variants. To follow up on the observations in the PGR, we conducted recall-by-genotype analyses of SLC30A8 LoF heterozygotes and homozygotes, as well as their participating family members, using OGTTs.
We identified 18 SLC30A8 knockouts, including homozygotes for a variant enriched in South Asians (Gln174Ter), and 1024 heterozygotes for LoF variants. Type 2 diabetes risk was lower in SLC30A8 LoF heterozygotes and homozygotes relative to non-carriers, and the protective effect strengthens in a gene dose-dependent manner (OR
The corrected insulin response, disposition index and insulin sensitivity index in LoF heterozygotes and homozygotes indicated higher glucose-stimulated insulin secretion with preserved beta cell function that was independent of BMI. These data suggest that therapeutic inhibition of SLC30A8, up to and including complete knockout, may treat type 2 diabetes safely and effectively.
Journal: Diabetologia
